article.title: The Psychology of Awe: How Nature Changes Our Minds

paragraphs.en: In modern society, daily life requires intense mental focus. Bright screens, loud traffic, and busy schedules constantly distract us from our inner thoughts. Furthermore, as cities grow and encroach upon natural spaces, people spend most of their time indoors. They often indulge in social media, a habit that psychologists note can lead to negative emotions like jealousy due to social comparison. However, scientific studies suggest that spending time in nature can solve many of these modern psychological problems.
paragraphs.zh: 在现代社会，日常生活需要高度集中的intense精神专注。明亮的屏幕、嘈杂的交通和繁忙的日程安排不断地分散distract我们对内心想法的注意力。此外，随着城市的发展并蚕食encroach自然空间，人们大部分时间都在室内度过。他们经常沉溺indulge于社交媒体，心理学家指出，由于社会比较，这种习惯会导致嫉妒jealousy等负面情绪。然而，科学研究表明，在自然界中度过时间可以解决许多这些现代心理问题。

paragraphs.en: In the 1980s, researchers Rachel and Stephen Kaplan introduced the "Attention Restoration Theory" (ART), which is now widely accepted in psychology. This theory explains that human attention is like a battery. When we are mentally tired, natural environments can restore our energy. Unlike a busy city street, an ordinary forest or park does not force our brains to process stressful information. There is a distinct difference in how our brains rest in green spaces compared to urban areas. Today, medical professionals broadly approve of spending time outdoors as a standard way to improve mental health.
paragraphs.zh: 在20世纪80年代，研究人员雷切尔和斯蒂芬·卡普兰提出了“注意力恢复restoration理论”（ART），该理论现在在心理学界被广泛接受。这个理论解释说，人类的注意力就像一块电池。当我们精神疲惫时，自然环境可以恢复restore我们的能量。与繁忙的城市街道不同，一个普通的ordinary森林或公园不会强迫我们的大脑处理有压力的信息。与城市地区相比，我们的大脑在绿地中休息的方式有明显的distinct不同。今天，医疗专业人员广泛认可approve将户外活动作为改善心理健康的标准方法。

paragraphs.en: But nature does more than just help us relax; it has a peculiar and fascinating effect on our social behavior. Recent research from the University of California, Berkeley, explores the emotion of "awe"—the feeling we get when we see something huge and amazing, like grand mountains or ancient trees. The researchers found that experiencing awe makes people less selfish. When someone feels small next to the vastness of nature, they are less likely to act like an egoist. Instead of focusing only on their own problems, they feel a stronger connection to others and a greater respect for human dignity.
paragraphs.zh: 但是，大自然不仅能帮助我们放松；它对我们的社会行为有一种奇特的peculiar和迷人的fascinating影响。加州大学伯克利分校最近的研究探索了“敬畏awe”的情感——这是当我们看到巨大而惊人的事物时，比如雄伟的山脉或古老的树木，我们所获得的感觉。研究人员发现，体验敬畏会让人变得不那么自私selfish。当一个人在大自然的广阔vastness面前感到渺小时，他们不太可能表现得像个自我主义者egoist。他们不再只关注自己的问题，而是感到与他人的联系更紧密，对人类尊严dignity的尊重也更深。

paragraphs.en: The results of these studies are clear: even if a person is only slightly exposed to natural beauty, they often show a more fervent desire to help strangers and cooperate with their community. In conclusion, the natural world is not just a place for physical exercise. It is a powerful psychological tool that heals our tired minds and makes us better, more connected human beings.
paragraphs.zh: 这些研究的结果是清楚的：即使一个人只是轻微地slightly接触自然美景，他们往往会表现出一种更强烈的fervent帮助陌生人和与社区合作cooperate的愿望。总之，自然界不仅是进行体育锻炼的地方。它是一个强大的心理工具，可以治愈我们疲惫的心灵，让我们成为更好、更有联系的人类。

intense.def: Of extreme force, degree, or strength.
intense.trans: 十分强烈的，高度集中的，紧张的

distract.def: To prevent someone from giving full attention to something.

encroach.def: To advance gradually beyond usual or acceptable limits.

indulge.def: To allow oneself to enjoy the pleasure of.

jealousy.def: The state or feeling of being jealous.

restoration.def: The action of returning something to a former owner, place, or condition.

restore.def: To bring back (a previous right, practice, custom, or situation).

ordinary.def: With no special or distinctive features; normal.

distinct.def: Recognizably different in nature from something else of a similar type.

approve.def: To officially agree to or accept as satisfactory.

awe.def: A feeling of reverential respect mixed with fear or wonder.

selfish.def: Lacking consideration for others; concerned chiefly with one's own personal profit or pleasure.

vastness.def: Very great extent or size; immensity.

egoist.def: A person who is preoccupied with their own interests; a selfish person.

dignity.def: The state or quality of being worthy of honor or respect.

slightly.def: To a small degree; not considerably.

fervent.def: Having or displaying a passionate intensity.

cooperate.def: To act jointly; work toward the same end.

chart.title: Impact of Environment on Prosocial Behavior
chart.description: Based on UC Berkeley research, this chart illustrates the shift in prosocial tendencies (such as willingness to help) after subjects are exposed to awe-inspiring natural environments versus ordinary urban settings.

insights.title: Urban Egoism
insights.text: In regular urban settings, individuals tend to focus significantly more on their own needs and internal thoughts.

insights.title: Nature's Awe
insights.text: Exposure to grand natural elements dramatically boosts a person's desire to cooperate and feel connected to the community.

insights.title: The Behavioral Shift
insights.text: Notice the sharp decrease in selfish focus and the concurrent rise in prosocial behaviors when subjects experience awe.

quiz.title: Knowledge Check
quiz.description: Test your comprehension of the B1/B2 vocabulary and the reading passage.

questions.q: According to the Kaplans' theory, while urban environments drain our mental energy, spending time in an ________ forest can help to ________ our attention.

rationale.en: The passage states that an 'ordinary' forest does not force stress on the brain, helping to 'restore' mental energy.
rationale.zh: 文章指出，一个‘普通的’（ordinary）森林不会给大脑施加压力，有助于‘恢复’（restore）精神能量。

rationale.en: Forests are described as ordinary, not eccentric, and their purpose in the theory is to restore attention, not distract it.
rationale.zh: 森林被描述为普通的，而不是古怪的，它们在理论中的作用是恢复注意力，而不是分散注意力。

rationale.en: Intense focus is caused by modern life, not a forest environment.
rationale.zh: 高度集中的注意力是由现代生活引起的，而不是森林环境。

rationale.en: While nature is appealing, the specific mechanism mentioned in the ART theory is 'restoration', not 'indulgence'.
rationale.zh: 虽然大自然很吸引人，但ART理论中提到的具体机制是‘恢复’，而不是‘沉溺’。

questions.q: The intense focus required by modern city life and the constant use of social media can lead people to ________ in habits that create negative feelings such as ________.

rationale.en: Social media habits generally do not restore dignity in this context.
rationale.zh: 在本文语境中，社交媒体习惯通常不能恢复尊严。

rationale.en: Awe is a positive emotion connected to nature, not a negative feeling from social media.
rationale.zh: 敬畏是与大自然相关的积极情绪，而不是来自社交媒体的消极情绪。

rationale.en: The text states that people often 'indulge' in social media, leading to negative emotions like 'jealousy' through comparison.
rationale.zh: 文本指出，人们经常‘沉溺’（indulge）于社交媒体，通过比较导致‘嫉妒’（jealousy）等负面情绪。

rationale.en: While social media distracts, 'egoist' is a noun for a person, not a negative feeling.
rationale.zh: 虽然社交媒体会分散注意力，但‘egoist’（自我主义者）指代的是人，而不是一种负面情绪。

questions.q: True or False: Medical professionals generally approve of the idea that outdoor activities are a valid method for improving psychological well-being.

rationale.en: The text explicitly mentions: 'Today, medical professionals broadly approve of spending time outdoors as a standard way to improve mental health.'
rationale.zh: 文本明确提到：‘今天，医疗专业人员广泛认可（approve）将户外活动作为改善心理健康的标准方法。’

rationale.en: The statement accurately reflects the passage.
rationale.zh: 该陈述准确反映了文章内容。

questions.q: True or False: When people experience the 'vastness' of nature, they are more likely to act like an egoist and focus exclusively on their own personal problems.

rationale.en: The passage says the exact opposite: people become less selfish and less like an egoist.
rationale.zh: 文章说的恰恰相反：人们变得不那么自私，也不太像自我主义者。

rationale.en: The text states: 'When someone feels small next to the vastness of nature, they are less likely to act like an egoist.'
rationale.zh: 文本指出：‘当一个人在大自然的广阔（vastness）面前感到渺小时，他们不太可能表现得像个自我主义者（egoist）。’

questions.q: What does the University of California, Berkeley research suggest about the peculiar effect of nature on human behavior?

options.text: It makes people slightly more distracted by the environment around them.

rationale.en: The research focuses on social behavior and awe, not just simple distraction.
rationale.zh: 该研究侧重于社会行为和敬畏，而不仅仅是简单的分心。

options.text: It creates an intense feeling of jealousy towards those who live in rural areas.

rationale.en: Jealousy is associated with social media in the text, not nature.
rationale.zh: 在文本中，嫉妒与社交媒体有关，而不是大自然。

options.text: It reduces selfish behaviors and creates a fervent desire to cooperate with others.

rationale.en: The passage concludes that exposure to natural beauty creates a 'fervent desire to help strangers and cooperate with their community.'
rationale.zh: 文章的结论是，接触自然美景会产生一种‘强烈的（fervent）帮助陌生人和与社区合作（cooperate）的愿望。’

options.text: It helps people maintain their dignity by avoiding social interactions entirely.

rationale.en: Nature actually encourages more social interaction and connection, not less.
rationale.zh: 大自然实际上鼓励了更多的社会互动和联系，而不是更少。
